title: Ambientconstruction
summary: Packages powers of the ambient Laplacian as weighted operators and proves their weight shift under the algebraic calculus interface.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages powers of the ambient Laplacian as weighted operators and proves their weight shift under the algebraic calculus interface.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows GJMSoperators/Ambientconstruction.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The ambient Laplacian power operator Δ̃^k. At this formal level, this is just the operator f ↦ lapPow k f. In explicit terms, for k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is the function sending f to Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian k f.
For k in the natural numbers, and f of type Function Conf, ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf k f equals Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian k f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianPowerOperator_apply (k : ℕ) (f : Function Conf) : ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k f = CalConf.lapPow k f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f of type Function Conf, ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf 0 f equals f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianPowerOperator_zero_apply (f : Function Conf) : ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf 0 f = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f of type Function Conf, ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf 1 f equals Cal Conf.lap f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianPowerOperator_one_apply (f : Function Conf) : ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf 1 f = CalConf.lap f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The operator Δ̃^k maps the GJMS input weight to the GJMS output weight. This is the weight bookkeeping theorem. It uses the already established homogeneity theorem for lapPow. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianPower_maps_GJMS_weight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : CalConf.MapsHomogeneousWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) (calculusOutputWeight CalConf k)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A slightly more explicit version of the previous theorem. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_maps_GJMS_weight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) {f : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) f) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (calculusOutputWeight CalConf k) (CalConf.lapPow k f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: If Δ̃^k is tangential at the GJMS input weight, then it defines a weighted tangential operator E[k - n / 2] → E[-k - n / 2]. In explicit terms, for h of type cal conf.algebraic identities, k in the natural numbers, and htangential of type cal conf.is tangential at weight ambient laplacian power operator cal conf k calculus input weight cal conf k, its defining expression is input weight : equals calculus Input Weight Cal Conf k output weight : equals calculus Output Weight Cal Conf k to Operator : equals ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf k maps Weight : equals ambient Laplacian Power maps GJMS weight Cal Conf H k tangential : equals htangential.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator}(H,k,htangential)=w_{\mathrm{in}} = w_{\mathrm{in}} \operatorname{CalConf} k w_{\mathrm{out}} = w_{\mathrm{out}} \operatorname{CalConf} k \operatorname{toOperator} = \operatorname{ambientLaplacianPowerOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} k \operatorname{mapsWeight} = \operatorname{ambientLaplacianPower\_maps\_GJMS\_weight} \operatorname{CalConf} H k \operatorname{tangential} = \operatorname{htangential}
correspondence.leanType: def ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)) : CalConf.WeightedTangentialOperator

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, k in the natural numbers, and htangential of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weight ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf k calculus Input Weight Cal Conf k, ambient Laplacian Power Weighted Tangential Operator Cal Conf H k htangential.input weight equals calculus Input Weight Cal Conf k.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;k\colon \mathbb{N},\;htangential\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeight} (\operatorname{ambientLaplacianPowerOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} k) (w_{\mathrm{in}} \operatorname{CalConf} k),\quad (\operatorname{ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} H k \operatorname{htangential}).w_{\mathrm{in}} = w_{\mathrm{in}} \operatorname{CalConf} k
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator_inputWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)) : (ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator CalConf H k htangential).inputWeight = calculusInputWeight CalConf k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, k in the natural numbers, and htangential of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weight ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf k calculus Input Weight Cal Conf k, ambient Laplacian Power Weighted Tangential Operator Cal Conf H k htangential.output weight equals calculus Output Weight Cal Conf k.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;k\colon \mathbb{N},\;htangential\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeight} (\operatorname{ambientLaplacianPowerOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} k) (w_{\mathrm{in}} \operatorname{CalConf} k),\quad (\operatorname{ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} H k \operatorname{htangential}).w_{\mathrm{out}} = w_{\mathrm{out}} \operatorname{CalConf} k
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator_outputWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)) : (ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator CalConf H k htangential).outputWeight = calculusOutputWeight CalConf k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, k in the natural numbers, and htangential of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weight ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf k calculus Input Weight Cal Conf k, ambient Laplacian Power Weighted Tangential Operator Cal Conf H k htangential.to Operator equals ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator_toOperator (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)) : (ambientLaplacianPowerWeightedTangentialOperator CalConf H k htangential).toOperator = ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Assuming the ambient Laplacian power is tangential at the GJMS input weight, package it as an abstract GJMS operator. In explicit terms, for h of type cal conf.algebraic identities, k in the natural numbers, and htangential of type cal conf.is tangential at weight ambient laplacian power operator cal conf k calculus input weight cal conf k, its defining expression is k : equals k to Weighted Tangential Operator : equals ambient Laplacian Power Weighted Tangential Operator Cal Conf H k htangential input Weight eq : equals rfl output Weight eq : equals rfl.
correspondence.leanType: def ambientLaplacianPowerAbstractOperator (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, k in the natural numbers, and htangential of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weight ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf k calculus Input Weight Cal Conf k, ambient Laplacian Power Abstract Operator Cal Conf H k htangential.k equals k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianPowerAbstractOperator_k (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)) : (ambientLaplacianPowerAbstractOperator CalConf H k htangential).k = k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, k in the natural numbers, and htangential of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weight ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf k calculus Input Weight Cal Conf k, ambient Laplacian Power Abstract Operator Cal Conf H k htangential.order equals 2 times k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianPowerAbstractOperator_order (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)) : (ambientLaplacianPowerAbstractOperator CalConf H k htangential).order = 2 * k

references.note: Original construction of conformally invariant powers of the Laplacian.

references.locator: Ambient Laplacian and homogeneous extension discussion
references.note: Ambient-metric background for the formal construction.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
